description_long: This object is a detailed model of a crustacean, likely a species of shrimp or prawn, characterized by a long, segmented body and two prominent claws of unequal size. The exoskeleton is a muted brownish-orange, with subtle gradations along the body and appendages. The cephalothorax is robust, tapering into a segmented abdomen that ends in a fan-shaped tail. Two long, slender antennae extend from the head, and multiple walking legs are visible beneath the body. The claws are asymmetrical, with one much larger and more elongated than the other, both featuring distinct pincers. The surface appears hard and slightly matte, with fine textural details visible on the shell and limbs.
description: A brownish-orange crustacean with a segmented body, long antennae, and two asymmetrical claws, featuring a hard, matte exoskeleton.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, the large claw extended forward, and the long antennae.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the symmetry of the body, the arrangement of legs, and the fan-shaped tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the smaller claw and the curvature of the body and tail.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the face, both claws, and the forward-projecting antennae.
